So essentially what we're going to do is take the client's target audience and map out the different personas that could make this audience up. ## [03:05 - 06:06]

Now you'll notice here under one of the personas, I entered generic broad because if you don't have a lot of personas or it's not, if it's not always clear what that could be, just go for the main big target audience, essentially. Now it's important to state here that some clients, some businesses that you work with are not going to have more than one persona. They've already niched down. It's very clear who they're targeting and who they're speaking to. That's fantastic that they know that. That's the whole purpose of running this first rapid fire test is to know who exactly you're going to talk to. So if you're working with a company that already has that and you don't have anyone else, you just have one persona, then skip to the second round of rapid fire testing. You don't need to do this. And you may also find that some clients will have two personas and some will have five and some will have 10 or 20 many, many, many personas. So it will vary based off the industry, the type of client and what their product is. So it's not like you, every client or every business is going to have three personas, not at all. You really have to dig into that. One of the things that helps to identify a persona is to ask the question, the solution that we're offering, which problems do they solve and for whom? Which problems do they solve and for whom? So that's a great way to figure out the personas within a main target audience. So if we break this down a little bit more, this is what it's essentially going to look like the setup. Okay. So we're going to run a campaign. This is going to be using CBO, Campaign Budget Optimization. We're also going to use Dynamic Creative. We're going to set it up as a conversion objective campaign. As you watch me set it up in the next video, I'm going to talk to you a little bit about if you don't have a sales page or a funnel to send to what campaign objectives to use for this. We're going to be spending between $20 to $100 a day. Now when we're setting this conversion objective campaign, we want to ideally optimize for the actual event, so a purchase or a lead or whatever it is that that funnel is for. Or if they don't have that or if they don't have enough data going through that, then what we'll do is place an event on the landing page and we're going to delay that event so that we know we're only essentially tracking and targeting high quality people, not just people that clicked an ad and bounced pretty much right away. Because if we optimize our campaigns based off those kinds of people, we're not heading
